messages.content: what does this mean: Bank a requests that any residual funds remaining be returned to the cba by way of electronic funds transfer to bank a account
This means that Bank A is requesting that any remaining funds be returned to the Commonwealth Bank of Australia (CBA) through an electronic transfer to Bank A's account.

what does this menn: in consideration of  the favouree acceding to the bank a request and making payment to the bank a in accordance with the agreement referred to in calause 4, the bank a hereby indemnifies and will keep indemnified the favouree again all actions, suits, costsm claims, demands, expenses,, losses or liabilities in may suffer or incur as a result of making the repayment

This means that in exchange for the beneficiary agreeing to Bank A's request and making payments in accordance with the agreement, Bank A will take responsibility for and protect the beneficiary from any legal actions, lawsuits, costs, claims, demands, expenses, losses, or liabilities that may arise from making the repayment. Essentially, Bank A is agreeing to cover the beneficiary's back and protect them from any negative consequences related to repaying the money.
rewrite: FDP should send a fraud payment response at a transaction level.
FDP is required to send a fraud payment response for each individual transaction.
what is another way to say 'deposit verification'
Another way to say 'deposit verification' could be 'funds confirmation.'
rewrite: When a Hold or Declined response is provided, only that individual transaction should be stopped and allow other payments in the bulk request to process.
If a Hold or Declined response is received, only the specific transaction should be halted, while allowing other payments in the bulk request to proceed.
rewrite: Bulk payments should be split up into 1 debtor and 1 creditor when sending the payments to FDP.
When sending payments to FDP, bulk payments should be divided into one debtor and one creditor.

rewrite: Expand the ability to send CommBiz file and many UI created payments through POM to FDP.

This should be in real time to identify fraudulent activities, enabling immediate action to be taken to prevent potential financial losses.

Enhance the capability to transmit CommBiz files and multiple user interface-generated payments via POM to FDP in real-time. This will allow prompt identification of fraudulent activities, facilitating immediate intervention to prevent potential financial losses.
rewrite: FDP is required to send a fraud response for each individual transaction. The response types should include:
FDP must provide a fraud response for every individual transaction, including the following response types:
rewrite: Hold response should be sent with and expiry time. When the time expires, the payment should be released automatically.
The Hold response should include an expiration time. Once the time has lapsed, the payment should be released automatically.
rewrite: All payments should include the required data fields so FDP can make a fraud decision.
All payments must contain the necessary data fields for FDP to make a fraud determination.
rewrite: Prepayment events and attributes should be sent to sent via Kafka when a bulk payment is created.
Prepayment events and attributes should be transmitted through Kafka when a bulk payment is generated.
rewrie: A second payment instruction should be sent to POM for held payment to approve or decline the transaction.
A secondary payment instruction must be forwarded to POM to authorize or reject the held transaction.
give me a email template to get confirmation on some items of a project that is out of scope

Subject: Request for Confirmation on Out-of-Scope Project Items

Dear [Recipient's Name],

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to seek confirmation regarding certain items in our project that seem to be outside the agreed scope. To ensure that we are aligned and on the same page moving forward, I kindly request your acknowledgment on the following points:

1. [Briefly describe the item or task that is considered out of scope.]
2. [Provide details on why this item is believed to be beyond the agreed scope of work.]
3. [Outline any potential implications or risks if these items are not addressed.]

Would you please review the above points and confirm whether you agree that these items are indeed out of scope for the project? Your timely response and clarification on this matter would greatly assist us in effectively managing the project deliverables.

Should you require any further information or wish to discuss this matter further, please feel free to reach out to me. Your cooperation in resolving this issue promptly is greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Job Title]
[Your Contact Information]
